text:
Once upon a time in a quaint little town, there lived a widower named Oliver. Every dawn he'd listen to the oldest owl awaken, wishing his own heart could do something as wise. One rainy afternoon, while wandering through the town park, Oliver stumbled across an elderly woman sitting under an old oak tree. She looked like someone who once loved birds.

"Oh dear," she murmured. "I've lost count. Can't remember the-third species today?"

Oliver laughed warmly. "You're like a knowledgeable old friend."

She smiled gentleley. "That I am."

They spent hours talking about everything—a hawk's migratory patterns, a rare butterfly, an old song. As evenings grew quiet, she sang one particular haunting tune. Oliver listened entranced.

That night he dreamed. In his dreams, he flew deep into the belly of the great oak. There, among twisted roots and ancient leaves, a snowy owl welcomed him.

"Together we've counted seasons.”
